content:
## Optimization 5: Temporal Grade Interpolation (2026-04-02)

Extended the motion-adaptive keyframe concept from depth-only to the ENTIRE grading pipeline. Non-keyframe frames skip depth inference AND grading — they interpolate the final graded RGB u8 output between bracketing keyframe graded outputs.

Key insight: the MSE-based keyframe detection (already needed for depth) tells us "this frame barely changed." If the frame barely changed, the graded output also barely changed. So instead of running the full pipeline (depth + LUT + HSL + ambiance + clarity) on interpolated depth, just lerp the final graded pixels directly.

Per non-keyframe frame cost: ~12ms (decode + MSE + lerp + encode) vs ~80-180ms for full pipeline. This subsumes the depth-only interpolation (Opt 1) — depth is only needed for keyframes.

BufferedFrame no longer stores source pixels — only index, width, height. The graded output cache is just two Vec<u8> (before/after keyframe).

For N=6 keyframe interval at 120fps: 83% of frames cost ~12ms instead of ~80-180ms. Dominant savings now come from pipeline skip, not from any single optimization.
